categories.label: Application
categories.description: Something end users interact with directly.

types.label: Web App
types.description: A website or web application users access in a browser.

fields.label: Who are the target users?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Small business owners, students, healthcare professionals...

fields.label: What are the key pages or views?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Dashboard, settings, user profile, search results...

fields.label: What authentication is needed?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Email/password, social login, SSO, none...

fields.label: Does it need to work on mobile browsers?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Yes - mobile-first, desktop only, both equally...

fields.label: Any real-time features?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Live chat, notifications, collaborative editing, none...

types.label: Mobile App
types.description: A native or cross-platform mobile application.

fields.label: Which platforms?
fields.placeholder: e.g. iOS, Android, or both...

fields.label: Native or cross-platform?
fields.placeholder: e.g. React Native, Flutter, Swift, Kotlin, native...

fields.label: Does it need to work offline?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Full offline mode, cached data, online only...

fields.label: Push notifications needed?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Yes - transactional alerts, marketing, none...

fields.label: Any device-specific features?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Camera, GPS, Bluetooth, biometrics, none...

types.label: Desktop App
types.description: An application that runs on Windows, Mac, or Linux.

fields.label: Which operating systems?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Windows, Mac, Linux, all three...

fields.label: What framework or approach?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Electron, Tauri, native Qt, .NET...

fields.label: Does it work fully offline?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Fully offline, syncs when online, requires internet...

fields.label: Any OS-level integrations?
fields.placeholder: e.g. File system, system tray, notifications, clipboard...

types.label: Mobile Game
types.description: A game designed for mobile phones and tablets.

fields.label: Which platforms?
fields.placeholder: e.g. iOS, Android, or both...

fields.label: What game engine?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Unity, Godot, Unreal, custom, undecided...

fields.label: What genre or style?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Puzzle, RPG, platformer, casual, strategy...

fields.label: Multiplayer or single player?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Single player, local multiplayer, online multiplayer...

fields.label: How will it make money?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Free with ads, in-app purchases, premium, freemium...

fields.label: Leaderboards or achievements?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Global leaderboards, achievements, tournaments, none...

types.label: CLI Tool
types.description: A command-line application run in a terminal.

fields.label: What commands does it need?
fields.placeholder: e.g. init, build, deploy, config, help...

fields.label: Interactive or scripted?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Interactive prompts, flags only, both...

fields.label: Config file format?
fields.placeholder: e.g. YAML, JSON, TOML, .env, none...

fields.label: How will users install it?
fields.placeholder: e.g. npm, pip, Homebrew, standalone binary...

categories.label: Service
categories.description: Backend software that other programs or services talk to.

types.label: REST API
types.description: A standard HTTP API with JSON endpoints.

fields.label: What are the main resources/endpoints?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Users, products, orders, payments...

fields.label: How will clients authenticate?
fields.placeholder: e.g. JWT tokens, API keys, OAuth2, none...

fields.label: Rate limiting needed?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Yes - per user/key, tiered limits, none...

fields.label: API versioning strategy?
fields.placeholder: e.g. URL versioning (/v1/), header-based, none yet...

types.label: GraphQL API
types.description: A flexible query-based API using GraphQL.

fields.label: What are the main types/entities?
fields.placeholder: e.g. User, Post, Comment, Organization...

fields.label: Real-time subscriptions needed?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Live updates for messages, notifications, none...

fields.label: How will clients authenticate?
fields.placeholder: e.g. JWT, session cookies, API keys...

fields.label: Pagination approach?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Cursor-based, offset, Relay-style connections...

types.label: Background Worker
types.description: A service that processes jobs, queues, or scheduled tasks.

fields.label: What triggers it?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Cron schedule, message queue, webhook, event...

fields.label: How often does it run?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Every 5 minutes, hourly, on-demand, continuous...

fields.label: How should failures be handled?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Retry 3 times with backoff, dead letter queue, alert...

fields.label: Logging and monitoring needs?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Structured logs, metrics dashboard, alerting...

categories.label: Infrastructure
categories.description: Cloud setup, DevOps pipelines, and platform engineering.

types.label: Cloud Platform
types.description: Cloud infrastructure, IAM, compute, storage, and networking.

fields.label: Cloud provider?
fields.placeholder: e.g. AWS, GCP, Azure, multi-cloud...

fields.label: Key services needed?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Compute, storage, database, CDN, functions...

fields.label: Scaling requirements?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Auto-scaling, fixed capacity, serverless...

fields.label: Budget or cost constraints?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Under $500/mo, optimize for cost, no limit...

fields.label: Source control platform?
fields.placeholder: e.g. GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket...

fields.label: What happens during a build?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Lint, test, compile, Docker build...

fields.label: Where does it deploy to?
fields.placeholder: e.g. AWS, Vercel, Kubernetes, self-hosted...

fields.label: What environments do you need?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Dev, staging, production, preview per PR...

categories.label: Library / SDK
categories.description: Reusable code that other developers consume.

types.label: Code Library
types.description: A reusable package that solves a specific problem.

fields.label: What language/runtime?
fields.placeholder: e.g. TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go...

fields.label: What problem does it solve?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Date formatting, validation, HTTP client...

fields.label: What's the public API look like?
fields.placeholder: e.g. 3-5 main functions, class-based, fluent builder...

fields.label: Versioning approach?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Semver, CalVer, breaking changes policy...

types.label: SDK
types.description: A developer kit for integrating with a platform or service.

fields.label: What platform/service is it for?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Your own API, payment provider, analytics...

fields.label: What languages should it support?
fields.placeholder: e.g. JavaScript, Python, Go, multiple...

fields.label: How do users authenticate/configure?
fields.placeholder: e.g. API key, OAuth, config file...

types.label: Framework
types.description: An opinionated toolkit for building a specific kind of application.

fields.label: What kind of apps does it help build?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Web apps, CLI tools, APIs, data pipelines...

fields.label: Core abstractions or concepts?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Components, middleware, plugins, hooks...

fields.label: Plugin or extension system?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Yes - plugin registry, middleware chain, none...

fields.label: Convention or configuration driven?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Convention over config, explicit config, hybrid...

full_product_modifier.label: Full Product
full_product_modifier.description: Add database, auth, security, integrations, DevOps, and monitoring layers on top of your project type.

fields.label: What kind of database?
fields.placeholder: e.g. PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, SQLite, multiple...

fields.label: Authentication requirements?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Social login, SSO, MFA, API keys, magic links...

fields.label: Third-party services to integrate?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Stripe, SendGrid, Twilio, AWS S3, none yet...

fields.label: Where will it be deployed?
fields.placeholder: e.g. AWS, Vercel, self-hosted, Docker, edge...

fields.label: Monitoring and alerting needs?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Error tracking, uptime monitoring, metrics dashboard, basic logs...

web-app.label: Web App
web-app.description: Frontend web application with state management.

mobile-game.label: Mobile Game
mobile-game.description: Mobile game with engine, platform, and monetization considerations.

background-worker.label: Background Worker
background-worker.description: Job processing, queues, or scheduled tasks.

code-library.label: Code Library
code-library.description: A reusable package that solves a specific problem.

sdk.label: SDK
sdk.description: Developer kit for integrating with a platform or service.

foundation.label: Foundation Layer
foundation.description: Boundaries, runtime assumptions, and API/contract foundation.

core-spec.label: Core Spec Layer
core-spec.description: Stabilize contracts, schema, and identity early to reduce downstream rework.

docs:full.label: Generate Documentation
docs:full.description: Create, draft, review, and verify all project documentation.

docs:release.label: Lock Documentation
docs:release.description: Verify and lock docs so they're ready for app scaffolding or handoff.

app:bootstrap.label: Scaffold Application
app:bootstrap.description: Generate the application skeleton from locked docs.

app:full.label: Build Application
app:full.description: Scaffold, build, and test the application from locked docs.

app:build-exec.label: Build with Exec Plan
app:build-exec.description: Scaffold the app, generate a build plan, execute it, then test.

app:ship.label: Deploy Application
app:ship.description: Deploy the application to the target environment.

system:full.label: Full Pipeline (End-to-End)
system:full.description: Run everything: generate docs, lock them, scaffold and build the app, then deploy and package.

system:import.label: Import Existing Project
system:import.description: Analyze an existing codebase and generate documentation from it.

export:package.label: Export Agent Kit
export:package.description: Package the locked documentation and artifacts into a downloadable ZIP.

draft.message: draft blocked unless seed complete for target module(s)

lock.message: lock refused unless latest verify is PASS

scaffold-app.message: scaffold-app blocked unless docs are locked (or explicit override 'dev_build')

deploy.message: deploy blocked unless tests PASS (or explicit override)
